title: PeeringDB Plus - Service Overview
description: Comprehensive observability dashboard for PeeringDB Plus covering sync health, HTTP RED metrics, per-type sync detail, Go runtime, and business metrics.

panels.title: Data Freshness
panels.description: Seconds since last successful sync. Green <1h healthy, yellow 1-2h delayed, red >2h indicates a problem.

panels.title: Sync Success Rate
panels.description: Should be near 100%. Drops indicate PeeringDB API issues or database write failures.

panels.title: Sync Duration (p95)
panels.description: 95th percentile sync cycle time. Normal range is 30-120s depending on data volume and mode (incremental vs full).

panels.title: Fallback Events
panels.description: Count of incremental-to-full sync fallbacks. Should be 0 under normal operation. Non-zero means incremental sync failed and a full re-fetch was triggered.

panels.title: Sync Operations
panels.description: Sync cycles by status (success/failed) and mode (incremental/full). If freshness is red, check this timeline for errors.

panels.title: Role Transitions
panels.description: LiteFS primary/replica role transition events

panels.title: Request Rate by Route
panels.description: HTTP requests per second grouped by route

panels.title: Error Rate (5xx)
panels.description: Ratio of 5xx server errors to total HTTP requests

panels.title: Active Requests
panels.description: Currently in-flight HTTP requests

panels.title: Latency p95 / p99
panels.description: HTTP response time percentiles across all routes

panels.title: Sync Throughput by Type
panels.description: Per-type upsert rate during sync windows. Network (net) and NetworkIxLan (netixlan) are typically the largest and slowest.

panels.title: Objects Synced per Type
panels.description: Total objects upserted per type over the dashboard's selected time range. Large counts are normal for types like netixlan (~100K+ objects).

panels.title: Deletes per Type
panels.description: Soft-delete tombstones marked per type per sync (Phase 68). Non-zero deletes are normal as PeeringDB data churns.

panels.title: Fetch / Upsert Errors
panels.description: PeeringDB API fetch errors and database upsert errors by type

panels.title: Goroutines
panels.description: Live goroutine count. Sustained growth is a leak signal.

panels.title: Heap Memory
panels.description: Go heap memory currently in use

panels.title: Allocation Rate
panels.description: Bytes allocated per second by the Go runtime

panels.title: GC Goal
panels.description: Target heap size for the Go garbage collector

panels.title: Total Objects
panels.description: Sum of all rows across 13 entity types. ~1.5M is the steady-state size.

panels.title: Objects by Type
panels.description: Object count per PeeringDB type

panels.title: Object Counts Over Time
panels.description: PeeringDB object counts per type over time

panels.title: Peak Heap
panels.description: Sync-cycle peak HeapInuse on the primary. Threshold line at 400 MiB — sustained breach re-fires SEED-001 (Fly 512 MB VM cap).

panels.title: Peak RSS
panels.description: Sync-cycle peak RSS on the primary (VmHWM from /proc/self/status). Threshold line at 384 MiB.

panels.title: Live Heap by Instance
panels.description: Per-VM Go heap from go_memory_used_bytes (OTel runtime gauge). Plots all fleet machines (primary + replicas) post-Phase-65 asymmetric fleet.

panels.title: Response Heap Delta — p50/p95/p99 by endpoint (Phase 71)
panels.description: Per-request Go HeapInuse delta on pdbcompat list handlers (/api/*) from pdbplus_response_heap_delta_bytes histogram. p50/p95/p99 quantiles over a 5-minute rolling window, grouped by endpoint. Shows 'No data' when there is no /api/ traffic in the selected window — the histogram is only observed on pdbcompat list requests, not on /healthz, /ui/, /rest/v1/, /graphql, or ConnectRPC.
